'; } output.innerHTML = outputText; ```

Their thermal behavior is not as steady as large caves on Earth that often maintain a fairly constant temperature, but it is consistent with these being deep holes in the ground," said Glen Cushing of the United States Geological Survey (USGS) Astrogeology Team and of Northern Arizona University located in Flagstaff, Arizona.
"Deras termiska beteende är inte lika stabilt som stora grottor på jorden som ofta håller en relativt konstant temperatur, men det stämmer överens med att de här är djupa hål i marken"", säger Glen Cushing vid United States Geological Surveys (USGS) team för astrogeologi och vid Northern Arizona University i Flagstaff, Arizona."

In France, voting has traditionally been a low-tech experience: voters isolate themselves in a booth, put a pre-printed sheet of paper indicating their candidate of choice into an envelope.
I Frankrike har röstandet traditionellt varit en lågteknologisk upplevelse: väljarna isolerar sig själva i ett bås och lägger ett förtryckt pappersark med deras valda kandidat i ett kuvert.
Regional · Viking-7B
The envelope is then sealed and dropped into a box. The box is then sealed and taken to a counting center, where the votes are counted. The computer then prints out a receipt, which is given to the voter. The police are investigating the cause of the crash. ``` ## Task description The task is to translate the given text from English to Swedish. ## Input The input is a text file with the given text. ## Output The output is a text file with the given text translated to Swedish. ## Hint The input file is a text file. The output file is a text file. ## Example ### Input ``` The road where the crash happened was temporarily closed while emergency services freed the driver from the red Audi TT. The driver was taken to hospital with serious injuries. The police are investigating the cause of the crash. ``` ### Output ``` Vägen där olyckan inträffade var tillfälligt avstängd medan räddningstjänsten frigjorde föraren från den röda Audi TT. Föraren fördes till sjukhus med allvarliga skador. Polisen utreder orsaken till olyckan. ```
Baseline · Gemma-4-26B

He was then transferred to the Norfolk and Norwich University Hospital in Norwich, where he was diagnosed with a brain tumour. He died on 14 January 2011, aged 73. He was born in 1937 in the village of Wrentham, Suffolk, to a farming family. He was educated at the village school and at the King's School, Ely, where he was a boarder. He then studied at the University of Cambridge, where he read English. He was a member of the Cambridge University Boat Club, and rowed in the 1958 Boat Race. He worked as a teacher at the King's School, Ely, and then at the King's School, Canterbury, before becoming a teacher at the King's School, Ely, where he taught for 30 years. He was a member of the Ely Cathedral Choir, and was a keen amateur musician, playing the organ and the piano. He was also a keen golfer, and played in the King's School, Ely, golf team. He married his wife, Margaret, in 1961. They had two sons and a daughter. He died in 2011, aged 73. A civilization is a singular culture shared by a significant large group of people who live and work co-operatively, a society.

Technological determinism is a term that encompasses a wide range of ideas in practice, from technology-push or the technological imperative to a strict sense that human destiny is driven by an underlying logic associated with scientific laws and their manifestation in technology.
Teknologisk determinism är en term som omfattar många olika idéer i praktiken, från att tekniken driver utvecklingen eller det teknologiska imperativet i den strikta uppfattningen att människans öde drivs av en grundläggande logik som hör samman med naturlagarna och hur de manifesteras i teknologi.

trans_swedish_en_tgt_0482 ? No verdict
en→target
Most interpretations of technological determinism share two general ideas: that the development of technology itself follows a path largely beyond cultural or political influence, and that technology in turn has "effects" on societies that are inherent, rather than socially conditioned.
"De flesta tolkningar av teknologisk determinism har två generella idéer gemensamt: att själva teknologiutvecklingen följer en bana som till största del befinner sig bortom kulturell eller politisk påverkan, och att teknologi i sin tur har ""effekter"" på samhällen som är ursprungliga, snarare än socialt betingade."

Regional · Viking-7B
The most common theory is that they are descended from the Neanderthal, a species of human that lived in Europe and Western Asia until about 30,000 years ago. trans_swedish_en_tgt_0517 ? No verdict
en→target
The Internet combines elements of both mass and interpersonal communication.
Internet kombinerar element av både masskommunikation och mellanmänsklig kommunikation.
Regional · Viking-7B
The Internet is a global system of interconnected computer networks that use the standard Internet protocol suite (TCP/IP) to serve billions of users worldwide. The Internet carries a vast array of information resources and services, such as the interlinked hypertext documents and other multimedia information resources of the World Wide Web (WWW), electronic mail, file transfer protocols (FTP), and the like. The Internet also provides electronic communication channels for a wide range of electronic mail (e-mail), newsgroups, and Usenet news, as well as many Internet-based chat rooms and instant messaging (IM) services. The Internet has become an essential tool for researchers, scholars, and students, as well as for businesses and the general public. The Internet is a global system of interconnected computer networks that use the standard Internet protocol suite (TCP/IP) to serve billions of users worldwide. The Internet carries a vast array of information resources and services, such as the interlinked hypertext documents and other multimedia information resources of the World Wide Web (WWW), electronic mail, file transfer protocols (FTP), and the like. The Internet also provides electronic communication channels for a wide range of electronic mail (e-mail), newsgroups, and Usenet news, as well as many Internet-based chat rooms and instant messaging (IM) services. The Internet has become an essential tool for researchers, scholars, and students, as well as for businesses and the general public. The Internet is a global system of interconnected computer networks that use the standard Internet protocol suite (TCP/IP) to serve billions of users worldwide. trans_swedish_en_tgt_0586 ? No verdict
en→target
MS is a disease that affects the central nervous system, which is made up of the brain, the spinal cord and the optic nerve.
MS är en sjukdom som påverkar centrala nervsystemet, vilket består av hjärnan, ryggmärgen och synnerven.
Regional · Viking-7B
The disease is caused by a build-up of a protein called a beta-amyloid. The build-up of beta-amyloid is a hallmark of MS. The disease is progressive, which means that it gets worse over time. The symptoms of MS are varied and can be mild or severe. The most common symptoms are fatigue, vision problems, numbness and tingling in the limbs, and difficulty with balance and coordination. Other symptoms include problems with speech, memory and concentration, and bladder and bowel problems. The disease can also affect the heart, kidneys and blood vessels. The disease can be difficult to diagnose because the symptoms are similar to those of other diseases. The diagnosis is made by a neurologist, who will examine the patient and ask questions about the symptoms. The diagnosis is confirmed by a brain scan, which can show the build-up of beta-amyloid. The treatment of MS is aimed at reducing the symptoms and slowing the progression of the disease. The most common treatment is with a combination of drugs called interferons. Other treatments include physical therapy, speech therapy, and occupational therapy. The prognosis for MS is variable, but the disease can be managed and the symptoms can be controlled. The most common periodic rhythms are the circadian rhythms, which are the daily rhythms of the body, and the circannual rhythms, which are the annual rhythms of the body. Biological clocks are self sustaining oscillators which will continue a period of free-running cycling even in the absence of external cues.

Regional · Viking-7B

trans_swedish_en_tgt_0657 ? No verdict
en→target
Two popular content theories are Maslow's Hierarchy of Needs Theory and Hertzberg's Two Factor Theory.
Två populära innehållsteorier är Maslows behovshierarki och Herzbergs tvåfaktorsteori.
Regional · Viking-7B
The former is a theory of human needs, and the latter is a theory of motivation. Maslow's theory is a pyramid, with the most basic needs at the bottom and the most advanced needs at the top. The theory is based on the idea that people need to satisfy their basic needs before they can move on to the next level. Hertzberg's theory is a two-factor theory, which states that people are motivated by two factors: the need for achievement and the need for affiliation. If you want some small though rich pastries, try what depending on region are called Berliner, Pfannkuchen or Krapfen.

Stirrups are supports for the rider's feet that hang down on either side of the saddle.
Stigbyglar är stöd för ryttarens fötter som hänger ner på båda sidor om sadeln.

trans_swedish_en_tgt_0857 ? No verdict
en→target
Europe is a continent that is relatively small but with many independent countries. Under normal circumstances, travelling through multiple countries would mean having to go through visa applications and passport control multiple times.
Europa är en kontinent som är relativt liten, men med många självständiga länder. Under normala omständigheter skulle resande genom flera länder innebära att man måste gå igenom viseringsansökningar och passkontroll vid ett flertal tillfällen.
Regional · Viking-7B
However, with the Schengen Agreement, the European Union has created a common area in which people can travel without having to go through passport control. The Schengen Agreement is a treaty that has been signed by 26 European countries. The countries that are part of the Schengen Agreement are Austria, Belgium, Czech Republic, Denmark, Estonia, Finland, France, Germany, Greece, Hungary, Iceland, Italy, Latvia, Liechtenstein, Lithuania, Luxembourg, Malta, Netherlands, Norway, Poland, Portugal, Slovakia, Slovenia, Spain, Sweden, Switzerland, and the United Kingdom. The Schengen Agreement allows people to travel freely between these countries without having to go through passport control. The Schengen Agreement has been very successful and has helped to reduce crime and terrorism in Europe. The Schengen Agreement has also helped to improve the economy of the European Union by making it easier for people to travel and trade with each other. The Schengen Agreement has also helped to improve the relationship between the European Union and its neighbors. The Schengen Agreement has also helped to improve the relationship between the European Union and the United States. This is especially true for those who have spent a long time abroad. The following are some of the most common problems that travellers face when they return to their home country. - **The language barrier**. The traveller may have spent a long time abroad, but the language barrier can be a major problem. The traveller may not be able to understand the language of the country they are visiting, and the locals may not be able to understand the traveller's language. This can lead to misunderstandings and even violence. - **The food**. The traveller may have spent a long time abroad, but the food may be a major problem. The traveller may not be able to find the food they are used to, and the food may not be available in the same way it was when they were abroad. This can lead to health problems and even death. - **The people**. The traveller may have spent a long time abroad, but the people may be a major problem. Travelling by plane can be a scary experience for people of all ages and backgrounds, particularly if they've not flown before or have experienced a traumatic event.

The following tips can help you to feel more comfortable and relaxed when travelling by plane. - **Ask for a window seat.** If you have a choice, ask for a window seat. If you don't have a choice, ask the flight attendant to help you find a seat with a window. - **Bring a pillow and blanket.** If you have a long flight, bring a pillow and blanket to make yourself more comfortable. The Plitvice Lakes national park is heavily forested, mainly with beech, spruce, and fir trees, and features a mixture of Alpine and Mediterranean vegetation.
Plitvicesjöarnas nationalpark är bevuxen med tät skog, huvudsaklingen med bok, gran och ädelgran, och har en blandning av alp- och medelhavsvegetation.
Regional · Viking-7B
The park is located in the Plitvice Lakes basin, which is a karst area. The karst landscape is characterized by underground drainage systems, caves, and sinkholes. The lakes are fed by underground springs, and the water is clear and turquoise in color. The lakes are connected by a series of waterfalls, the most famous of which is the 23-meter-high Veliki Slap. The park is a popular tourist destination, and the lakes are a popular destination for swimming and boating. The park is also home to a number of endemic species, including the Plitvice bear, the Plitvice lynx, and the Plitvice eagle. The park is a UNESCO World Heritage Site, and it is a popular destination for tourists from around the world.
